blurb:
Cody got adopted and came back. Not because anything went wrong, but because his family loved him and still couldn't keep up with him. He's a three-year-old cattle dog mix at about 33 pounds, and he has the energy to prove it.

The volunteers describe him as "a strong and lovable guy with a sweet nature," and that tracks. He's friendly with other dogs, he did well at doggie daycare in his previous home, and he's a Prison Pups program graduate. He knows his commands. He calms right down for a bath and a good rub. He's not chaotic. He just needs a real outlet.

He can be a little mouthy during play, which is pretty standard for cattle dog mixes and something he's working on. He's strong enough that toddlers could get accidentally knocked over, so a household with older kids is a better fit. Cats are unknown since he hasn't had a real introduction, but the rescue can work through a meet and greet if you have pets at home.

If you run, hike, bike, or just have a big yard and someone at home most of the day, Cody will reward that. He's not a couch dog looking for a quiet house. He's a good dog looking for someone who can match him.

Reach out to Mostly Mutts through their website or by phone to set up a meeting.
shelter_hours: Check mostlymutts.org for current hours and appointment availability
scoring_notes:
• Prison Pups graduate returned through no fault of his own is a clean, sympathetic pitch for active adopters and outdoor lifestyle households
• Energy-forward profile with honest mouthiness disclosure builds reader trust and targets a specific but sizeable active-family demographic
• Returned once with enough foster and program time logged to suggest a moderate wait, creating mild but real placement urgency

blurb:
Eight years old and still melting hearts. Glynnis is a medium-sized terrier mix who came in through animal control and has spent her time since then doing foster stays, getting loved on by volunteers, and even completing the Mostly Mutts Prison Pups training program. She is not killing time. She is getting ready.

The volunteers call her "the sweetest dog ever" and say she just melts into you for scratches. She gives kisses, she dreams in her sleep with little kicks and sounds, and she'll let you know when someone's at the door. Gentle, cuddly, easy-going. That's Glynnis.

She does have some preferences worth knowing. She's shown she likes male dogs more than female dogs, and she's a vocal player, so a chill male dog at home who won't be thrown off by that energy would be ideal. She did try to chase a cat in a foster home, so cats are probably not the right fit. Kids ten and up should do great with her. She did well with a couple of boys at the shelter and didn't miss a beat.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw, and you can start the process by submitting an inquiry through their website or giving them a call. Ask about Glynnis by name.
shelter_hours: Check mostlymutts.org for current hours and appointment availability
scoring_notes:
• Prison Pups graduate with volunteer-endorsed 'sweetest dog ever' praise gives editors a credible, heartwarming hook
• At eight years old with animal-control origins and multiple foster stays, time-in-system signals a long road deserving spotlight urgency
• Specific dog and child preferences narrow placement but Prison Pups training frames her as a prepared, not difficult, candidate

blurb:
Cecelia has already been adopted once. She came back after nine months, and when she returned she was shut down, scared, and clearly carrying something heavy. That was then. She's in a foster home now and doing great.

Here's who she actually is once she trusts you: whole-body wags. Lap dog energy in a 37-pound terrier-boxer mix. Her foster family says she's the perfect housemate, which is a pretty different story from "high energy" label she got in her previous home. She knows sit, stay, come, and leave it. She's working through a training program. She is putting in the work.

She does need the right situation. She'd rather be your only dog at home since she tends to guard her person. She was cautious around strangers at first, especially men, and reaching over her head or grabbing at her before she's comfortable will spook her. None of that is a dealbreaker. It just means she needs someone patient enough to let her come to them. Give her that, and she's going to crawl in your lap and stay there. Mostly Mutts recommends kids 15 and older.

Cecelia is at Mostly Mutts in Canton. Submit an inquiry through their website to get started, and let her foster tell you more about who she's becoming.
shelter_hours: Check mostlymutts.org for current hours and appointment availability
scoring_notes:
• Comeback arc — returned shut-down and scared, now a whole-body-wag lap dog — is the most emotionally resonant transformation story in this batch
• Obedience training investment and foster family's 'perfect housemate' endorsement reframe her trauma history as a strength, not a red flag
• A second return after nine months plus recovery time in foster care points to one of the longer cumulative shelter journeys here, demanding reader action

blurb:
Someone found Strawberry and her siblings outside without their mom when they were barely old enough to have their eyes open. Mostly Mutts took them in, lined up foster homes, and now Strawberry is 2.5 months old, about two pounds, litter trained, and ready to go.

The shelter volunteers put it pretty simply: sweet and playful as she is adorable. She grew up with her siblings so she's comfortable around other cats and would likely do great with a friendly cat already in your home. She goes after wand toys, crinkly balls, and the cat tunnel in the shelter's kitty room. Then when she runs out of energy, she's a lap cat. That's the whole day.

Her kitten vaccines are started but you'll need to finish the series as she grows. Adoption fee applies and Mostly Mutts can walk you through the details when you reach out.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Head to their website to submit an adoption inquiry and meet Strawberry in person.
shelter_address: 3080 Kennesaw Due West Rd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30152
scoring_notes:
• Kitten with lap-cat personality and play drive is among the easiest possible adoption pitches for any audience
• Rescue-from-the-street origin is warm but familiar, offering modest narrative lift without a truly standout hook
• Recent intake as a young kitten suggests she arrived recently and is unlikely to have waited long at all

blurb:
Scully came into Mostly Mutts from animal control alongside another kitten named Mulder. Yes, they named them after the X-Files duo. No, that's not the reason to adopt her, but it doesn't hurt.

She's 2.5 months old and about two pounds, and according to the volunteers she follows you around like a tiny, curious shadow. Whatever you're doing, she wants to know about it. She chases crinkly toys, pounces on things that aren't there, and explores every corner of a room before eventually winding up in your lap. She gets along well with the other cats she's met at the shelter, so if you've got a cat at home who could use a playmate, she's worth a look.

Her kitten vaccines are started and you'll finish the series as she grows. She's microchipped. She's too small to be spayed yet, but it's required and Mostly Mutts covers that cost once she's big enough.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Visit their website to submit an inquiry and set up a time to meet her.
shelter_address: 3080 Kennesaw Due West Rd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30152
scoring_notes:
• X-Files naming hook is an instant reader smile and gives the newsletter a fun, shareable angle beyond the typical kitten blurb
• Shadow-personality with lap-cat payoff is a highly adoptable profile that works for families and singles alike
• At 2.5 months and microchipped with neuter coverage included, she is a fresh intake — low wait-time urgency

blurb:
Twiggy has already done this once. She was adopted from Mostly Mutts years ago, found a family she loved, and then a new cat joined the household and things didn't work out. No drama, no bad behavior — just a tortoiseshell who prefers to be the one and only. Now she's seven years old, about 12 pounds, and looking for round two.

She was a little spicy when she first came back in. That's fair. Her world got flipped upside down and she needed time to decompress. She's been in a foster home since then, settling back into herself. What her previous owner knows for sure: once Twiggy trusts you, she loves hard. She wants attention. She wants to be part of your life. She just needs to be the star of it.

She'd do best as your only cat. A dog might be okay depending on the dog, and older kids who know how to be calm around cats could work too. She's fully vaccinated, microchipped, combo tested negative, and spayed. Adoption fee is $175.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Submit an inquiry through their website to get connected with her foster and set up a meet.
shelter_address: 3080 Kennesaw Due West Rd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30152
scoring_notes:
• Returned adopter with a loyal, loving track record makes her a relatable second-chance story for readers
• Solo-cat requirement narrows the pool but her fully vetted status and foster stability signal she's ready now
• Seven-year-old returnee with decompress time behind her signals a longer shelter journey that adds urgency to feature
